programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A couple ignore warnings to leave their home but call in the paranormal team when one of them awakes with a cross painted on their forehead

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The anglers travel to Australia in search of barramundi, a fish that is almost impossible to find in the wild - until their Aboriginal guides take them to a barra paradise

programme.title: Blue Serengeti
programme.desc: Shark expert Barbara Block gives a portrait of the Blue Serengeti, three marine hotspots off the coast of California that have been made more visible thanks to new technology

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Russell records thermal imagery that believes is Bigfoot. Mireya and Ronny have a terrifying experience when they realise they are not alone in the forest

programme.title: Surviving Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Joe Exotic gives one of his last interviews before his arrest. Plus, ex-employees reveal the truth about his infamous zoo, and there is a look at the rescue of some of its big cats

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts answer more questions about the animal kingdom, including whether dogs feel guilty when they do something wrong and why tigers are orange

programme.desc: Tia looks back at the highlights of season four. Also featuring a look behind the scenes, and updates on the dogs featured this season

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Maryam, who is a pet psychic but cannot communicate with her vicious cat Lily. Khloe discovers an entire feral colony behind her parents house

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps families calm their unruly felines, meeting Debbie who is torn between her boyfriend and her incontinent cat Blinkie. Plus, Krisi and Jason want to get married, but standing in their way is her energetic feline Frankie

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Mike Boone relocates an alligator snapping turtle, Inman and Eddins get a poacher to confess, while Provaznik educates fishermen on the Texas coast

programme.desc: Dr Jeff and Dr Petra work together to save a beloved pet huskys leg. Specialist Dr Baier has a scare during the routine treatment of a pet pig

programme.desc: John Fulton travels to Americas west coast. In Los Angeles, he samples a brew made from coffee beans scooped out of cat excrement

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe explains how watching scenes from nature can help bring feelings of positivity, happiness and wellbeing to the viewer

programme.title: A Night on the Town
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a photographer and an advertising designer trying on clothes in a vintage clothing store find themselves transported to the 1930s. Musical showcasing the work of Irving Berlin, Noel Coward, George Gershwin and Cole Porter, starring Hinton Battle, Lewis Collins, Frank Gorshin, Eartha Kitt, John Moffatt, Elaine Paige, Ann Reinking and Bobby Short. First aired in 1983

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke looks at art from one of Ancient Egypts most opulent eras, beginning with the troubling psychological portraits of tyrant Senwosret III, who ruled during the Middle Kingdom, through to the golden mask of Tutankhamun, which was created hundreds of years later. He also explores architectural wonders, exquisite tombs and a lost city, where an artistic revolution took place and a new creative style was developed under Akhenaten and Nefertiti

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson presides over another duel of words and wits as team captains Frank Muir and Patrick Campbell are joined by guests Lesley-Anne Down, Anthony Valentine, Jilly Cooper and Quentin Bell. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Paul Jennings and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: Pompeii: The Mystery of the People Frozen in Time
programme.desc: Documentary in which Margaret Mountford visits the Roman town of Pompeii, where in 79AD the catastrophic eruption of Vesuvius encased its citizens in lava, creating body casts that populate the ruins. No one has yet been able to fully explain how these people became frozen in time, so while forensic scientists peer beneath the plaster to rebuild the faces of two victims and recreate the final moments of the townsfolk, Margaret dispels the myths surrounding the events in 79AD and explores the lives of the individuals who lived in the enigmatic city

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Internationally renowned abstract artist Frank Bowling became the first black Royal Academician in 2005. As a major retrospective opens at Tate Britain, Brenda Emmanus meets him to look back over his long career and there are interviews with critics and fellow artists to discuss the significance of his work in the history of British art

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: Visits to historic places of work, including one of Britains oldest dockyards in Chatham Kent, and Armley Mill in Leeds. At a 1,000-year-old tithe barn in Sussex, the steeplejack tries his hand at thatching, before getting a surprise on the top floor of the architecturally esteemed Lloyds building in the City of London

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: Businesswoman and Dragons Den investor Sara Davies explores the Swinton Estate in North Yorkshire. Along the way Sara reveals the challenges she faced as a young businesswoman, the unexpected impact of participating in Strictly Come Dancing and the importance of family support

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Paul Jennings and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke looks at art from one of Ancient Egypts most opulent eras, beginning with the troubling psychological portraits of tyrant Senwosret III, who ruled during the Middle Kingdom, through to the golden mask of Tutankhamun, which was created hundreds of years later. He also explores architectural wonders, exquisite tombs and a lost city, where an artistic revolution took place and a new creative style was developed under Akhenaten and Nefertiti

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: Visits to historic places of work, including one of Britains oldest dockyards in Chatham Kent, and Armley Mill in Leeds. At a 1,000-year-old tithe barn in Sussex, the steeplejack tries his hand at thatching, before getting a surprise on the top floor of the architecturally esteemed Lloyds building in the City of London

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: Businesswoman and Dragons Den investor Sara Davies explores the Swinton Estate in North Yorkshire. Along the way Sara reveals the challenges she faced as a young businesswoman, the unexpected impact of participating in Strictly Come Dancing and the importance of family support

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is appalled when she spots a strange man wrapped in only a towel exiting Elizabeths house next door. Convinced that it is her moral duty to save her friend from this scandal, Hyacinth begins to plot ways of flushing the man out

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.sub-title: Does Your Mother Know Youre Out
programme.desc: Mother wants Timothy to paint the cupboard under the stairs before the man comes to read the meter. However, Timothy is more concerned with betting snide colleague Victor that he can take new library assistant Caroline for a cinema trip to see Gone With the Wind

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian reflects on the impact of the Black Death, a disease that ravaged 14th-century England and wiped out half the population, leaving decades of anarchy and civil unrest for the survivors. The plague, which sowed the seeds of the Peasants Revolt, ironically contributed to the rise of the country gentleman

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Exploring a three-way relationship and a radically different approach to fatherhood. In Norway, trans people were subjected to forced sterilisation between 1979 and 2016. Kris, who has has always wanted to be a father, refuses to let that injustice define his future. Alongside Sindre, David and their extended families, Kris embarks on a journey that challenges traditional models of family and parenthood

programme.title: Dawn Chorus: The Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: Filmed without voiceover or music, this programme highlights the call of the birds at sunrise, as those living within Britains habitats burst into song and reveal the natural splendour of their distinctive chorus

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack explores the achievements of Britains civil engineering pioneers, visiting the Channel Tunnel, Humber Bridge and Pontcysyllte Aqueduct. He also has a go at legging a narrowboat through a canal tunnel in Dudley in the West Midlands

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Nina Wadia unwinds in the spring setting of Swaledale in the Yorkshire Dales National Park, where she reflects on personal family challenges, including the loss of her parents. Nina also meets an ornithologist who shows her some of the rarer bird species found in the area, and tests her skills in a new language when bumping into a village elder who speaks Swardle, the historic local dialect

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Exploring a three-way relationship and a radically different approach to fatherhood. In Norway, trans people were subjected to forced sterilisation between 1979 and 2016. Kris, who has has always wanted to be a father, refuses to let that injustice define his future. Alongside Sindre, David and their extended families, Kris embarks on a journey that challenges traditional models of family and parenthood

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Angela Rippon hosts the satirical quiz, with comedian Jo Brand and writer and broadcaster Richard Osman joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the weeks news stories

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Four precious Amur tiger cubs are growing fast, but with only around 400 left in the wild, every step of their journey will be critical. The challenge for the keepers today is weighing the cubs, without causing stress or triggering aggression from their fiercely protective mum, Yana. Meanwhile, theres drama in Animal Adventure as Longleats century-old tortoises wake from their deep winter slumber

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Fields of Danger as Farmer Is Impaled and Eagles Threaten Highland Flocks
programme.desc: Documentary following the police, fire and ambulance services as they attend emergency situations in rural communities. The East Anglian Air Ambulance races to save a farmer after a freak accident, lambs face attack from birds of prey, and reporter Paul Colgan investigates illegal fly-tipping in Cheshire. Featuring accounts from first responders, community members and farmers at the centre of these emergencies

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: The Flying Squad hunt for a brazen gang stealing jewellery and cash across the UK. And a daring heist of valuable rare books sets police off on an international manhunt

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Owain and Dianne Buswell
programme.desc: Strictly champion Dianne Buswell joins the team in this celebrity special, helping Owain Wyn Evans with the sale of a south London terrace, while Dion Dublin is in West Yorkshire, where he encounters some worrying-looking mould. Plus, a segment from the archives in which Martin Roberts visits a former chapel in Derbyshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents a round-up of highlights from the series featuring wooden antiques, catching up with red and blue teams across the country who bought some memorable pieces

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball reveals how cutting smartphone use to just two hours a day can aid wellbeing, offering a busy beautician in Kettering some clever digital detox tricks

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Pensioner David has decided to sell his rare and unique circus memorabilia and put the money raised towards a good cause - entertaining locals with his clown and variety act and helping support his poorly wife. After being greeted by a knights suit of armour, auctioneer Christina Trevanion makes museum-quality finds among Davids collection, including items that once belonged to famous circus owner PT Barnum. Meanwhile, JJ Chalmers finds an important and iconic piece of British innovation lying forgotten in a garden shed.

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Prestwicks art deco Broadway Cinema has not shown a film since its doors closed in 1976, so Mark Stuckey and Steve Rowley must battle rust, missing wires and obsolete carbon-arc lamps if they hope to bring its twin projectors back to life. Dom Chinea meets Shetlander Victoria, who needs her grandfathers accordion fixed if shes to realise her busking ambitions, and then discovers the centuries-old art of enamelling watch dials, a craft that demands steady hands

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.sub-title: Dangerous Dogs: Is The Ban Working? - Panorama
programme.desc: Two years ago, the Government banned XL Bully dogs, promising it would save lives. Reporter Sam Poling investigates whether that promise has been kept. programme.desc: Steve Backshall takes a boat ride up the Amazon to come face to face with a black piranha. He also encounters electric eels, meets a maned wolf and braves crocodile-infested waters

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: A day trip in memory of Conors dad leads the gang to an encounter with a landowner that sparks pandemonium. To add to the chaos a mysterious amount of cash has also appeared, so Mairead and an interfering Billy Murphy jump to conclusions

Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.desc: Desperate to impress his crush, awkward fresher Rohan turns to an online masculinity coach for advice, but begins to question if alpha status is all its cracked up to be

programme.desc: An aspiring comedian must choose between sanitising his set and embracing his voice after a controversial performance results in further gigs being cancelled

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse travel overseas for an Irish fishing trip on the River Finn, their first time angling in the waters of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland as they try to catch salmon. The Emerald Isle has some of the most prolific river runs for salmon in Western Europe, and its wet climate provides some of the best conditions for salmon fishing

programme.title: Chronicles of the Sperrins
programme.desc: Caroline from Conservation Detection Dogs NI along with Monty the spaniel are on a special mission to sniff out the location of an extremely rare native plant, which botanists are keen to study and protect. For night sky hunter Martin, summer sees him waiting hours to see if any magical noctilucent clouds will appear

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience in Clacton-on-Sea, Essex

programme.title: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programme.desc: The inside story of how America and China have become locked in a battle for global power and influence since Donald Trump first took office. The documentary begins with Trump inauguration in 2017, when he upended the USs relationship with Beijing. He promised to make China pay - but when he started a trade war, Xi Jinping was ready to fight back

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Clive Myrie meets distillery manager Luke in Bristol to explain how swapping his coffee and energy drinks for tea could lower his stress levels, reduce anxiety, calm the brain and support heart health

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: The team is in Clackmannanshire hoping to revive Alloa Co-op Bowling Clubs faded and warped sign in their centenary year. Meanwhile, Dom Chinea delivers a 19th-century regulator clock from the Hebrides with a ghostly connection, and Will Kirk heads north to Rothes in Moray to learn the craft behind forging traditional copper stills for the multibillion-dollar whisky industry

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents from Shepton Mallet, Somerset as Raj Bisram and Thomas Forrester help two teams find profitable antiques to sell at auction. Friends and neighbours Pat and Linda, lead by Raj Bisram, have sparkly items on their shopping list but are taken by a flamingo-headed walking cane. Sisters Jess and Jo, guided by Thomas Forrester, are looking for something that jumps out at them from the get-go

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Bike aficionado Tim Gunn tries to make a bent unicycle roadworthy once more for Heather from Winchester, who bought it as a gift for her late husband Perry. Mark Stuckey is entrusted with a canary yellow 1980s cassette player that belonged to owner Florences mum Mel. Other items are an Edwardian brass plate camera and a one-of-a-kind 3-D model of an adenovirus - the virus responsible for the common cold - made by an electron microscopist back in the 1960s

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quickfire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: The newsreader is enthusiastic about taking a dip in the vast volcanic spring of Saturnia and discovering why Italians believe in the healing power of thermal water. Travelling on through Maremma - a big stretch of country running between Tuscany and Lazio - Clive stays in a treehouse at a lavender farm and learns how to extract oil from the plants. He also joins Italys last surviving cowboys, known as the Butteri, to learn how to lasso

programme.title: Big Little Journeys
programme.desc: In Madagascar, a Kirindy forest chameleon emerges from its egg and starts the journey of a lifetime to find a mate. Will she lay her eggs before time runs out? In the Highlands of Scotland, a water vole is preparing to raise a family, but the scent of an approaching invasive American mink causes her to flee and abandon her home

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and his roving team of experts value more antiques and rarities in Lochgilphead, Argyll and Bute, including a pair of Staffordshire zebras, a painting by Scottish artist Hamilton McKenzie, a carved bone ship made by Napoleonic prisoners of war, and a painted asbestos tile by Kurt Schwitters produced in a Second World War internment camp

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Anita Manning value items brought to Blackburn Cathedral. Anita discovers an Omar Ramsden spoon, and Charles finds an old silver bucket that has been used as a bin and a Christmas tree holder but turns out to be Faberge. Presenter Paul Martin learns about how mill workers in Blackburn during the Industrial Revolution were woken by a human alarm clock known as a knocker upper

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: Rick rediscovers his love for Argyll and Bute, meeting chef Pamela Brunton, whose menu exhibits local and foraged ingredients. He catches langoustine with teacher-turned-fisherwoman Mary Galloway, explores a century-old smokehouse, and at home he cooks arroz rojo and tattie scones with Scottish smoked salmon

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: I Tackled a Terrorist With a Narwhal Tusk
programme.desc: Sculptor Nick attempts to create a piece honouring civil servant Darryn, who fought off a knife-wielding terrorist with a narwhal tusk in London in 2019. Bill Bailey believes Nick is the perfect artist to make a sculpture that honours both the hero and the human behind the headlines

programme.title: Mastermind

The contestants are then tested on their general knowledge before the winner is revealed

programme.title: University Challenge

programme.desc: Part two of two. Xi Jinpings government have high hopes for Joe Biden, Donald Trumps successor as US president, but these are dashed after a frosty meeting in Alaska. Biden sparks further anger in Beijing when he signs a nuclear submarine deal with Australia and the UK, and takes on China over tech, before Nancy Pelosis visit to Taiwan brings the two superpowers closer than ever to military confrontation

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate

programme.title: Weather

programme.desc: Producer, arranger, musician and composer Neil Martins collaborations with artists and friends made from a lifetime of music, following him through celebration, grief and new ambitions to ask whether the work of being an artist ever feels finished. In Irish

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian visits Romania. Landing in Bucharest, the only place in the country hes heard of, Romesh meets political activist and media expert Angela-Mihaela Serban at the parliament palace, the architectural behemoth built on the command of the communist dictator Nicolai Ceausescu. Failing to warn him about the miles theyre about to clock, she takes Rom out of the capital to explore many amazing things that are overlooked in this vast slice of Eastern Europe, and the pair embark on a journey through the countrys seemingly endless and varied landscapes

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A look at life after dark on the North York Moors. At the Dark Skies Festival, Margherita Taylor finds out how light pollution is affecting nocturnal wildlife, while Sammi Kinghorn meets the people gathering vital dark-sky data and installing wildlife-friendly lighting to help protect the night. Plus, Adam Henson meets apprentices at a cattle mart meeting

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: The documentary investigates the immediate fallout from the kidnap of John Cantlie and James Foley, as a perilous manhunt was launched by an expert hostage negotiator. But as the country collapsed, Islamic State took hold - and as more journalists joined them in captivity, survival became a desperate struggle

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: When Britain and the US refuse to negotiate with Islamic State, James Foley pays the ultimate price at the hands of Jihadi John. After the execution is streamed worldwide, John Cantlie reappears in a very different propaganda video. He is alive and apparently reporting on behalf of the extremists, forcing the world to question what side he is on and how far he must go to stay alive

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Owain and Dianne Buswell
programme.desc: Strictly champion Dianne Buswell joins the team in this celebrity special, helping Owain Wyn Evans with the sale of a south London terrace, while Dion Dublin is in West Yorkshire, where he encounters some worrying-looking mould. Plus, a segment from the archives in which Martin Roberts visits a former chapel in Derbyshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents a round-up of highlights from the series featuring wooden antiques, catching up with red and blue teams across the country who bought some memorable pieces

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.sub-title: When You Find Out You Have 468 Wax Melts
programme.desc: The team arrive in the Midlands to help dads Craig and Craig, whose home is jam-packed with toys belonging to their beloved five-year-old daughter - not only the ones she plays with now, but everything she has ever owned, which salon owner Big Craig has kept hold of for fear of losing part of her childhood. But as the process begins, Stacey finds more clutter, from almost 200 pairs of shoes to 400 tampons - which Little Craig collected for the homeless but never got round to donating

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive makes gelato and learns why it is healthier than ice cream, gets a makeover from Florences most stylish man, and stays in a hotel that is a homage to Italian design

programme.title: Monkey Planet
programme.desc: Zoologist George McGavin explores the world of primates, from strange lemurs and acrobatic monkeys to enigmatic apes. He uncovers the secrets of the primates success - their survival tactics and physical adaptations - and how their lives mirror those of their human cousins. He begins by meeting an orang-utan that uses soap to improve its personal hygiene, baboons with a taste for flamingos, criminal-minded macaques and fluorescent mandrills that don warpaint before going into battle

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team travel to Salford, Greater Manchester, where they cast their eyes over a drawing by local artist LS Lowry, Zulu wedding beads, an American scrimshaw whales tooth and an album of Victorian photographs

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Royal Marines Museum in Hampshire. Visitors arrive with their antiques and collectables and the experts are on hand to choose the most interesting items to go to auction. Paul Martin finds about the history of the Royal Marines

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: In Lincolnshire Rick meets inventor James Dyson, who allows him special access to his futuristic farm, where robots tend to strawberries all year round. Chef Rachel Green cooks Rick traditional plum bread, he also meets pig farmers producing Lincolnshire sausages. He then cooks strawberry sorbet and bangers and mash

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Guest judge Patrick Grant joins Diarmuid and Carol as they explore three more contenders for Northern Irelands Greatest Garden. They visit a coastal sanctuary in Millisle, a 15-acre woodland and wildlife garden in Ballymoney, and a country cottage with herbaceous borders and a cut flower garden in Greyabbey

programme.title: EastEnders

The family tackle an overflow of toys, clothes, storage cubes and some strong emotions as they work to get past their what if mentality and make a manageable home that works for them

programme.desc: Scientists study claims of a dead mermaid discovered and preserved in Japan. Plus a glowing-eyed sea creature captured on video chasing a motorboat and the connection between a man who lived alone in the jungle for over a quarter of a century and a fabled lost city in the Amazon

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Alien Sabotage, Ghost Child and Hungry Hungry Sasquatch
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things caught on film. A species of carnivorous marsupials, thought to be long extinct, are suspected of being caught on camera. Plus, the notion that a visitor at a historic cemetery in Savannah captured a ghost child

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.desc: Tony Harris breaks down the latest developments and most compelling photographic evidence in a tumultuous year of UFO revelations

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the medieval history of Lincoln, visiting the cathedral that was once the tallest building in the world and re-living the battle at Lincoln Castle. She discovers how moving up the social ladder in medieval times required a whole new wardrobe of clothing and accessories and gets top grips with a historically accurate crossbow. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson uses the latest drone technology to look at the geographical features that shaped the citys development

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores Londons Restoration history, including the founding of the Royal Society and the violent dark side behind Charles IIs carefully crafted public image. She visits St Pauls Cathedral, reveals how women performed on stage for the first time during this period, and uncovers shocking details of the slave trade at the National Maritime Museum. Plus, a look at parallels between the plague that struck London during the 1660s and the Covid-19 pandemic

programme.desc: George Clarke helps more people make their period homes fit for modern life. On a cobbled street in Cheshire sits a Georgian town house that could be straight out of a Dickens novel. It may look very 18th-century on the outside, but inside George discovers that its a 1980s horror show, having been stripped of its original features. It does have one unique feature, though - a first-floor conservatory on stilts. Can he find a way of getting its grandeur back? George also has his work cut out tackling a 380-year-old Jacobean cottage

programme.title: Channel 4 News

Emma is juggling shift work, clubs, cooking and a painful spinal condition, while Matts long shifts mean he rarely cooks. Their hectic schedule has left them short on time, short on energy and spending almost £400 a week on food, with 22 shopping trips in a week

programme.title: Sphinx

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Emmas new shop is flourishing - and so is romance with the handsome David. But love has let her down before, and she finds herself torn between following her heart and revenge on the Fairleys. Meanwhile, a newly sober Adele crashes back into Fairley Hall, jeopardising Adam and Olivias affair and Edwins upcoming marriage to Priya. In the 1970s, Emma commands her granddaughter Paula to break off her romance with Jim Fairley, forcing Paula to choose between her grandmother and her lover

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: At Fairley Hall, death has left a void. Grief-stricken Gerald is in serious debt to a mysterious lender, with the Fairley mill tied up as collateral. Emma has settled into her marriage to Joe Lowther and works tirelessly to be a brilliant wife and mother but theres tension in the household; Joe knows her work will always come first. While Emma prepares the perfect Christmas for her family, her unrelenting obsession with work and revenge brings danger to her door. In the 1970s, as Emma recovers from a heart attack, an oblivious Paula doubles down on her love affair with Jim

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In the seaside town of Weymouth, Dorset, the presenter helps a couple whose first-time family home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood. When they bought their 19th-century coach house in 2018, they had planned to renovate its higgledy-piggledy layout, but the overnight disaster means theyve got a far bigger project than they bargained for. The architect also visits Chiswick, west London, to assist a couple whose master bedroom and bathroom he redesigned a couple of years ago. With a growing family, next on their demolition list is the pokey kitchen

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds

programme.title: Countdown

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Mother Nature wreaks havoc as heavy rain prevents Richard and Simons bar in Portugal from opening and brings work outside of Mark and Garys gîte in France grinding to a halt. In Italy, boiler problems at Lisa and Pauls holiday apartment bring disarray to their first booking

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Married couple Ami and Mark host the weeks second visit at Kits Coty Glamping near Aylesford in Kent. Some of the BandBers are hailing their huts at the plush glampsite - but alarm bells are ringing for Emma Louise and Liv in their bell tent. After a history tour in the afternoon, its the hosts who are wanting to consign some cleanliness comments to the past

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On night two in Sheffield, law lecturer Sally serves up an authentic old school Shanghai menu. The group all dress to impress but Ambrose is worried that Sally might be his biggest competition and has got a sweat on. He tries to contain his glee when he finds a bit of twine in his starter but is otherwise impressed by her presentation and flavours. Everyone sees a new side to Sally when she reveals an enormous tattoo that runs down her leg and her talents seem to have no end as she showcases her piano skills on the spinet

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: The enormity of the project hits home hard as Jasmine and Jon begin renovations on their Costa del Sol home. programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A Great Dane called Scooby comes into Woodgreen looking for a new friend to take home and the Davies family have a double date with Moana the Labrador and Buffy the German shepherd puppy. Fox the stray pomeranian has a second chance at adoption

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britains most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. After 40 years living largely in the southeast of England, artist Sarah is returning to her roots in the northeast with towering ambitions for an exposed, three-acre plot that shes bought at auction in the Durham Dales. She wants to create a radical home and creative hub combined, including a studio and gallery for displaying and selling art. With her budget of £330,000, Sarah hopes to build with a super-sustainable yet still relatively unknown and hard-to-predict material - hempcrete. Left exposed throughout the interior, the hempcrete will provide a dramatic earthy look, but this relies on the building process going perfectly. But, just a few months into Sarahs eight-month schedule, her plan to move in before winter is scuppered as her inexperience as a project manager starts to show. With material costs going through the roof, and her budget doing likewise, selling her one-bed holiday cottage looks like the only way to keep building. As the critical hemp process gets going, and proves increasingly tortuous and unpredictable, the success of Sarahs project hangs in the balance

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Seven players remain, all vying to win up to £100,000. But with five Predators and only two Prey, the competition must undergo a reset. As the rules of the game take a radical new direction, theres a brutal surprise for one player and a dramatic reversal of fortune for another. But its an agonising, heart-breaking decision forced onto one Prey that provides the biggest upset of the game so far

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics

programme.desc: George Clarke helps more people make their period homes fit for modern life. On a cobbled street in Cheshire sits a Georgian town house that could be straight out of a Dickens novel. It may look very 18th-century on the outside, but inside George discovers that its a 1980s horror show, having been stripped of its original features. It does have one unique feature, though - a first-floor conservatory on stilts. Can he find a way of getting its grandeur back? George also has his work cut out tackling a 380-year-old Jacobean cottage

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Emmas new shop is flourishing - and so is romance with the handsome David. But love has let her down before, and she finds herself torn between following her heart and revenge on the Fairleys. Meanwhile, a newly sober Adele crashes back into Fairley Hall, jeopardising Adam and Olivias affair and Edwins upcoming marriage to Priya. programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: At Fairley Hall, death has left a void. Grief-stricken Gerald is in serious debt to a mysterious lender, with the Fairley mill tied up as collateral. Emma has settled into her marriage to Joe Lowther and works tirelessly to be a brilliant wife and mother but theres tension in the household; Joe knows her work will always come first. While Emma prepares the perfect Christmas for her family, her unrelenting obsession with work and revenge brings danger to her door. In the 1970s, as Emma recovers from a heart attack, an oblivious Paula doubles down on her love affair with Jim

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In the seaside town of Weymouth, Dorset, the presenter helps a couple whose first-time family home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood. When they bought their 19th-century coach house in 2018, they had planned to renovate its higgledy-piggledy layout, but the overnight disaster means theyve got a far bigger project than they bargained for. The architect also visits Chiswick, west London, to assist a couple whose master bedroom and bathroom he redesigned a couple of years ago. With a growing family, next on their demolition list is the pokey kitchen

He tries to contain his glee when he finds a bit of twine in his starter but is otherwise impressed by her presentation and flavours. programme.title: The Dog House

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britains most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. After 40 years living largely in the southeast of England, artist Sarah is returning to her roots in the northeast with towering ambitions for an exposed, three-acre plot that shes bought at auction in the Durham Dales. She wants to create a radical home and creative hub combined, including a studio and gallery for displaying and selling art. With her budget of £330,000, Sarah hopes to build with a super-sustainable yet still relatively unknown and hard-to-predict material - hempcrete. Left exposed throughout the interior, the hempcrete will provide a dramatic earthy look, but this relies on the building process going perfectly. But, just a few months into Sarahs eight-month schedule, her plan to move in before winter is scuppered as her inexperience as a project manager starts to show. With material costs going through the roof, and her budget doing likewise, selling her one-bed holiday cottage looks like the only way to keep building. As the critical hemp process gets going, and proves increasingly tortuous and unpredictable, the success of Sarahs project hangs in the balance

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Seven players remain, all vying to win up to £100,000. But with five Predators and only two Prey, the competition must undergo a reset. As the rules of the game take a radical new direction, theres a brutal surprise for one player and a dramatic reversal of fortune for another. programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Boob Job

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Memorial Service
programme.desc: A joke entry on Rosss college website backfires on Chandler. Joey refuses to give Emma his favourite stuffed toy, and weak-willed Phoebe begs Monica to help her avoid contacting Mike. Comedy, starring Matthew Perry and Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone except Ross pools their money to buy lottery tickets, but a disagreement about how to spend any winnings prompts a disdainful Phoebe to make a rash gesture. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel joins Joey on the set of his soap opera, where a steamy love scene inspires unexpected feelings - and a disturbing dream. programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Soap Opera Party
programme.desc: Joey throws a party for the cast of Days of Our Lives, but refuses to invite his pals in case their behaviour embarrasses him. However, the answering machine gives the game away and everyone attends - which is good news for Ross

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Fertility Test
programme.desc: Chandler and Monica go to a fertility clinic for tests, but are embarrassed to encounter an old acquaintance. Ross helps Joey pretend to be intelligent to woo Charlie, even though he is attracted to her himself, and Rachel sneaks into a spa for a massage

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler conduct an embarrassing interview with a potential sperm donor after learning they cannot conceive a baby naturally. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Double bill. The gang visits Barbados to hear Ross address a convention, but Chandler manages to destroy the only copy of his keynote speech. programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Joey and Rachel Kiss

programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Is Fine

programme.title: Friends

James Camerons action comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Jamie Lee Curtis and Tom Arnold

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Motor Pickers
programme.desc: Paul and Helen hunt down the best bargain coupes for a driver who wants luxury, comfort and space for his golf clubs. They find options to fit his £12,000 budget

programme.title: Motor Pickers

programme.title: Outback Truckers

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: On a trip to Worcestershire, Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove fall in love with a barn full of extraordinary automobilia. However, the guys find it hard work to negotiate with the vendors

programme.title: Shed and Buried

programme.title: Richard Hammonds Workshop

programme.title: Outback Car Hunters

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Classic Cars

programme.title: Outback Truckers

programme.title: Motor Pickers

programme.title: Motor Pickers

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Shed and Buried

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Bike guru Henry Cole and ace mechanic Sam Lovegrove look around a shed full of vintage motorcycles and intriguing memorabilia. The guys see potential in a Yamaha RD350, but can they afford it?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: British people may be famous for a stiff upper lip, but divorcees Shirlie and Dave get up close and personal. Its clear these fiftysomethings are feeling frisky. Richard and Freya bond over a shared love of musical theatre, and 33-year-old mother-of-two and yoga teacher Abi says she is looking for no less than James Bond. Will nice guy Mark leave her shaken or stirred?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including Squid Game: The Challenge, Im a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!, The Crown, Deal or No Deal, Planet Earth III, Made in Chelsea and Saving Lives at Sea. From November 2023

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics sit down to watch Squid Game: The Challenge - the real-life version of the Netflix hit - and get hot under the collar with Channel 4s swinging drama The Couple Next Door. Plus, The Toy Hospital, Survivor, Vinnie Jones in the Country, Tony Robinsons Marvellous Machines and Lorraine. From December 2023

programme.title: Monster Hunter (2020)
programme.desc: When Cpt Artemis and her loyal soldiers are transported to a new world, they engage in a desperate battle for survival against enormous enemies with incredible powers. Fantasy adventure based on the video game, starring Milla Jovovich, Tony Jaa and Ron Perlman

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Tired of the familys obsession with electronic gadgets, Claire and Phil declare a household challenge to see who can stay unplugged the longest. Mitch and Cameron frantically try to get Lily into the best pre-school, while Jay and Manny are concerned at the lengths to which Gloria will go to stop the neighbours dog barking

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Mercedes is determined to find out what Jensons up to, Ste makes a big decision, and a sister is backed into a corner. Warren decides who he wants, but is he too late? Friends gather around to support Diane and Tony, Rex braves his first public outing while experimenting with his image. At The Loft relaunch party, a shocking turn of events puts all eyes on Mercedes

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Warren decides who he wants, but he may be too late, and friends gather around to support Diane and Tony

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Rex braves his first public outing while experimenting with his image at an LGBTQ+ club night, and at the Loft relaunch party, a shocking turn of events puts all eyes on Mercedes

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Comedy clips showcasing amusing internet videos featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay travels to the El Greco restaurant in Austin, Texas, where the owners have resorted to using a microwave to prepare their authentic Greek dishes. Gordon faces the difficult task of uniting a squabbling family before tackling the bigger problem of a business on the brink of collapse

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Geoff struggles to balance demands from the Jenkintown Posse and Erica. Meanwhile, Adam faces a crisis of confidence over his career path

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As Valentines Day approaches, Barry is shocked when an attractive teaching assistant agrees to go out with him. Meanwhile, Adam and Dana worry that theyve missed their chance with their new crushes

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Luke spend father-son time together searching for hidden treasures beneath the house, Mitch and Cam cause embarrassment by inviting Lilys paediatrician to lunch, and Haley takes her driving test

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil finds himself in an awkward situation when he meets his former high-school girlfriend, who confesses she still has feelings for him, while Mitchell stands up to his boss and Jay summons up the courage to confess he killed Mannys turtle

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The dinner party descends into chaos when a shock return re-ignites old feuds and exposes fresh cracks in multiple marriages

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A sexual health adviser with two soon-to-be-married daughters goes looking for love, and a part-time singer enjoys an evening with a professional magician. Other visitors to the restaurant include an accountant contemplating parenthood, a car enthusiast who has been single for seven years, and a call-centre worker who has yet to fall in love

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family-run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michons owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now its down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michons from disaster

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Principal Skinner uses his Counter Truancy Unit to thwart a possible terror attack at Springfield Elementarys Annual Bake Sale. Rogue agent Bart could be the only hope of stopping the plot - until he clashes with Jack Bauer, who is on an important mission of his own. With the voices of 24s Kiefer Sutherland and Mary-Lynn Rajskub

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: You Kent Always Say What You Want
programme.desc: Homer accidentally causes the sacking of newsreader Kent Brockman - who moves in with the family and sets about trying to win his job back by conducting a high-profile campaign against unethical media practices. With the voice of rapper Ludacris

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Cake-maker and self-confessed princess Fiona seeks someone chunky and funky, finding herself paired with recruitment consultant Rakim. Ex-soldier Tristan from Exeter is on a mission to find his perfect woman after being single for four years. Will beach-loving Ema get swept away by his passion for the south west? Twenty-nine-year-old Lewis - whose narcolepsy caused him to fall asleep on a previous date - is paired with DJ Thom, while lager and football-lover Jessica is introduced to fellow northerner and pub-lover Ryan

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Owners Greg and Mary Russell met and fell in love while they were working there, and the place held such sentimental importance to them they pooled their money and bought it. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Trans man Charlie hopes to use his Irish charm to win the affections of 22-year-old Charlotte, who claims to have no gender boundaries. programme.desc: Anna Richardson guides contestants through the game of choosing a partner naked, as they must whittle six contenders down to one with whom to share a date. She also returns to the fertility clinic where her frozen eggs and embryos are being stored, confronting the reality of her ticking body clock

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Trans man Charlie hopes to use his Irish charm to win the affections of 22-year-old Charlotte, who claims to have no gender boundaries. Retired market trader Billy (82) has his work cut out trying to tame divorced aromatherapist Sandra, and 20-year-old Rhys struggles to get a word in when he sits down for a date with chatty nanny Georgia

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson guides contestants through the game of choosing a partner naked, as they must whittle six contenders down to one with whom to share a date. Here, sick of the party lifestyle, 27-year-old former club rep Chris, from London, hopes to meet someone to settle down with. Next up is 27-year-old technophobe Louise, from Buckinghamshire, who hasnt so far embraced the modern way of dating. A fan of classic romantic novels, Louise is looking for an old fashioned gentleman to sweep her off her feet

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson guides contestants through the game of choosing a partner naked, as they must whittle six contenders down to one with whom to share a date. This edition features Ryan, from Hull. Born with cystic fibrosis, the 24-year-old martial arts instructor is hoping to meet someone who shares his desire to live life to the max

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Two new couples enter the experiment but, when big personalities clash, the new marriages threaten to unravel before the honeymoons have even begun

programme.title: Vicky Pattison: Maybe, Baby?
programme.desc: Part one of two. Vicky and her husband Ercan decide whether they should start a family. In this first episode of two, Vicky throws herself into finding out what life with a child might be like by helping to look after a friends young daughter. She also returns to the fertility clinic where her frozen eggs and embryos are being stored, confronting the reality of her ticking body clock

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: With Dave Kims parents out of town for the weekend, Adam decides to throw a giant party to impress Brea. Beverly lets fame go to her head after her cookbook is published

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam is thrilled when William Penn institutes a new open lunch policy, but Beverly has other plans. Geoff gets more than he bargained for when he uses Barrys student ID

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Billy believes the stys the limit as he plans a pig-themed wedding, wanting to use the occasion to put his village Mansfield Woodhouse on the map with a world-record pig parade. Wheher his prim and proper bride Steph will kick up a stink remains to be seen

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits a restaurant specialising in barbecued food in Hendersonville, Tennessee

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits a husband and wife in Nashville who run a gourmet food truck serving unique versions of the grilled cheese sandwich

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: The chef travels to South Wales, where he is accompanied by Michelin-starred chef Stephen Terry. Here, James cooks a Welsh lamb ragu on a beautiful mountainside

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Miami where an Asian-fusion restaurant serves up Brussels sprouts and a duck sous-vide sandwich

programme.title: Man v Food with Casey Webb
programme.desc: Casey Webb ventures to Roswell, New Mexico for a UFO-inspired Thanksgiving sandwich. Later, he takes on the undefeated 10-98 challenge

programme.title: Man v Food with Casey Webb
programme.desc: Casey Webb heads to Boulder, Colorado for a bourbon burger blast and campfire classic. Plus, he samples an insanely hot pizza with three layers of ghost pepper

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: Simon King and Dave Myers are visiting the stunning Peak District. Starting in the world-famous Chatsworth House, the pair head through the village of Heage, home to one of Britains last windmills still in use, before setting up base in the manor house of Harthill Hall. They cook a traditional breakfast, some spicy patties and a picnic made of scotch eggs, custard tarts and some sweet cookies.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree hosts a girls night at the lodge. On the menu is a spread of canapes including citrus shrimp nachos and courgette, and quinoa cakes with herby yoghurt

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares the recipe for her pork schnitzel sandwiches with warm red cabbage, and spicy stir-fry Kung Pao cauliflower

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gives some of the nations favourite dishes a twist, reinventing British classics like shepherds pie, rustling up outdoor treats and transforming old-school desserts such as pavlova and sticky-toffee pudding. In the first edition, the chef takes on three comfort foods at his home in Essex, and hes joined by friend Christian, aka DJ BBQ, to reveal his ultimate burger - including what cut of meat to use, how to cook it and what to have on the side. Plus, Jamies secret sauce recipe

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver is joined by his friend, the Italian chef Gennaro Contaldo, to make bolognese ravioli, and also creates the ultimate cheese toastie as well as his take on the nations favourite dish - chicken tikka masala - cooked over an open fire

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: American chef Ina Garten prepares lobster salad with lemon mayonnaise, and learns how to make torte au chocolat at the Michelin-starred Auberge du Soleil

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: American chef Ina Garten enjoys a casual dinner in the garden and prepares Israeli couscous and tuna salad, with raspberry crumble bars for dessert

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc present as the remaining four bakers take a deep breath and head into the semi-final of the nations favourite baking contest. However, theres no time to marvel at how far theyve come - its Patisserie Week, and they will need to demonstrate that they have skills worthy enough to see them through to this years final. The bakers are challenged to make a signature baklava - two types of any flavour they like, before the technical challenge demands they knock up a German Schichttorte, a cake cooked in stages under the grill to create 20 layers of different coloured sponge. Finally, the showstopper sees the semi-finalists baking non-stop to create two entremets in which they should demonstrate as many personal skills and techniques as they can

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Three contestants remain and only one more weekend of baking stands between them and the 2014 title. Judges Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry have saved their most fiendish tests till last, as the finalists face a Signature Challenge in which they have just three hours to prove they have mastered a pastry technique that usually takes a whole day. They then have to tackle a Technical Challenge without the aid of a recipe, before rustling up a Showstopper that turns sponge, caramel, choux pastry and petits fours into a winning combination. At least Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc are on hand to lighten the atmosphere if it all gets too tense

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James goes on a culinary road trip around the UK and kicks off his travels in the Orkneys, where he is joined by fellow chef Nick Nairn. He shops for hand-dived scallops, before setting up his mobile kitchen in Stromness harbour to make barbecue scallops with black pudding, apple and hazelnut butter. He also meets the owner of a unique herd of sheep in the remote island of North Ronaldsay

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The host and travelling companion Nick Nairn continue their Scottish adventure in Lossiemouth, home to the first RAF squadron and a fleet of typhoon fighter jets

programme.title: Marcus in the Med: Mallorca
programme.desc: Marcus explores the food Mallorcans eat on the go. He tries pizza-style coca de trampo, then heads to a local market for a quick, fresh lunch

programme.title: Marcus in the Med: Mallorca
programme.desc: Marcus discovers what Mallorcans like to eat at special occasions. He makes his version of a local favourite, almond cake, and a jug of rose sangria

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: The celebrated chef takes a culinary romp around south-east England, sampling whelks in Bognor Regis, cooking a traditional steak-and-kidney pudding, exploring the herbal delights of a walled garden in Sussex and ending with a mission to save the sea bass

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: The chef visits East Anglia, Lincolnshire and the Midlands. He samples a host of regional specialities including Norfolk black turkeys, Lincolnshire chine, pork pies and Stilton. programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The bikers explore the county of Tyne and Wear and the city of Newcastle Upon Tyne, where Simon King has spent most of his life. They cook a roast dinner - a porchetta with all the trimmings, including Yorkshire puddings and a spiced apple sauce. The next day, Kingy takes Dave Myers to experience the food renaissance thats happening in Newcastle. Eventually they round things off with a visit to the Angel Of The North

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares her quick, crowd-pleasing pasta recipes. She makes lemon pasta, grilled steak pasta and drunken spaghetti with red wine

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares some of her favourite cooking-free recipes. She adds a spin to her ranch muffuletta sandwich, Chinese chicken salad and ice cream

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gives more of the nations favourite dishes a twist, making sticky toffee pudding with his mother Sally and rustling up a mouth-watering steak and onion sandwich. The chef also prepares an Italian classic - osso buco with saffron risotto

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver puts his stamp on three comfort food classics. First up is a retro treat - chicken Kiev, before the chef prepares spaghetti vongole with a non-traditional pink twist and a deluxe shepherds pie made with slow-roasted lamb shoulder rather than mince

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten prepares fig and fennel caponata, an antipasto platter and Tuscan mashed chickpeas for the team who built her barn

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten prepares raspberry muffins and coffee for breakfast, before heading to local restaurant South Fork Kitchen

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve contestants begin the battle to be named Britains best amateur baker, facing tasks devised by judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, who have created 30 new tasks to test their baking skills and creativity. Their first signature challenge is to make a Madeira cake, before they attempt to bake a frosted walnut cake in the technical. Day two of the contest sees the contestants going all out to try and impress the judges with their first showstopper, the Black Forest gateau. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins present

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: With the cake week completed, and one of their number already eliminated from the competition, the 11 remaining home bakers must now prove their skill at making biscuits, a task that can be deceptively difficult, and one that has undone many a confident Bake Off contestant in the past. The opening signature challenge this week is to create 24 biscotti, Italian delicacies that must be baked twice to deliver the perfect crisp crunch. Next is Paul Hollywoods technical challenge, in which the contestants tackle Arlettes - labour-intensive, puff pastry-based treats from southwest France. Finally, for the showstopper, the judges demand bespoke edible biscuit boxes, filled with 36 biscuits of different flavours. After all three rounds have concluded, it falls to Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins to announce which of the bakers will be asked to leave the tent

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The chefs culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands, where he takes a tour of the grounds and cooks venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Later, James gets a masterclass in Highland gaming as two young brothers David and Harry share the secrets to the famous caber toss

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and his companion Tom Kitchin head to Edinburgh, but first they take a detour to a whisky distillery in Pitlochry, where James knocks up whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once they reach the capital, Tom shares his knowledge of the local fish, James gets fitted for a kilt and learns the history of the iconic garment, and the pair enjoy a bite to eat at Toms Michelin-starred restaurant

programme.desc: Jamie Oliver prepares a hearty fish pie, helped by his son Buddy, with a fish curry using coconut milk and spinach for the sauce. programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Popular chef Jamie Olivers latest menu features ham, egg and chips with a Jamaican jerk rub and caramelised pineapple, chicken kebab served with houmous and mango amba sauce, and a three-tiered chocolate sponge cake complete with nougat frosting

programme.desc: Three contestants remain and only one more weekend of baking stands between them and the 2014 title. Judges Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry have saved their most fiendish tests till last, as the finalists face a Signature Challenge in which they have just three hours to prove they have mastered a pastry technique that usually takes a whole day. They then have to tackle a Technical Challenge without the aid of a recipe, before rustling up a Showstopper that turns sponge, caramel, choux pastry and petits fours into a winning combination. At least Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc are on hand to lighten the atmosphere if it all gets too tense

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James goes on a culinary road trip around the UK and kicks off his travels in the Orkneys, where he is joined by fellow chef Nick Nairn. He shops for hand-dived scallops, before setting up his mobile kitchen in Stromness harbour to make barbecue scallops with black pudding, apple and hazelnut butter. He also meets the owner of a unique herd of sheep in the remote island of North Ronaldsay

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The host and travelling companion Nick Nairn continue their Scottish adventure in Lossiemouth, home to the first RAF squadron and a fleet of typhoon fighter jets

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: Simon King and Dave Myers are visiting the stunning Peak District. Starting in the world-famous Chatsworth House, the pair head through the village of Heage, home to one of Britains last windmills still in use, before setting up base in the manor house of Harthill Hall. They cook a traditional breakfast, some spicy patties and a picnic made of scotch eggs, custard tarts and some sweet cookies.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree hosts a girls night at the lodge. On the menu is a spread of canapes including citrus shrimp nachos and courgette, and quinoa cakes with herby yoghurt

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares the recipe for her pork schnitzel sandwiches with warm red cabbage, and spicy stir-fry Kung Pao cauliflower

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gives some of the nations favourite dishes a twist, reinventing British classics like shepherds pie, rustling up outdoor treats and transforming old-school desserts such as pavlova and sticky-toffee pudding. In the first edition, the chef takes on three comfort foods at his home in Essex, and hes joined by friend Christian, aka DJ BBQ, to reveal his ultimate burger - including what cut of meat to use, how to cook it and what to have on the side. Plus, Jamies secret sauce recipe

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver is joined by his friend, the Italian chef Gennaro Contaldo, to make bolognese ravioli, and also creates the ultimate cheese toastie as well as his take on the nations favourite dish - chicken tikka masala - cooked over an open fire

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: American chef Ina Garten prepares lobster salad with lemon mayonnaise, and learns how to make torte au chocolat at the Michelin-starred Auberge du Soleil

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: American chef Ina Garten enjoys a casual dinner in the garden and prepares Israeli couscous and tuna salad, with raspberry crumble bars for dessert

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc present as the remaining four bakers take a deep breath and head into the semi-final of the nations favourite baking contest. However, theres no time to marvel at how far theyve come - its Patisserie Week, and they will need to demonstrate that they have skills worthy enough to see them through to this years final. The bakers are challenged to make a signature baklava - two types of any flavour they like, before the technical challenge demands they knock up a German Schichttorte, a cake cooked in stages under the grill to create 20 layers of different coloured sponge. Finally, the showstopper sees the semi-finalists baking non-stop to create two entremets in which they should demonstrate as many personal skills and techniques as they can

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Three contestants remain and only one more weekend of baking stands between them and the 2014 title. At least Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc are on hand to lighten the atmosphere if it all gets too tense

programme.title: Marcus in the Med: Mallorca
programme.desc: Marcus explores the food Mallorcans eat on the go. He tries pizza-style coca de trampo, then heads to a local market for a quick, fresh lunch

programme.title: Marcus in the Med: Mallorca
programme.desc: Marcus discovers what Mallorcans like to eat at special occasions. He makes his version of a local favourite, almond cake, and a jug of rose sangria

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: The celebrated chef takes a culinary romp around south-east England, sampling whelks in Bognor Regis, cooking a traditional steak-and-kidney pudding, exploring the herbal delights of a walled garden in Sussex and ending with a mission to save the sea bass

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: The chef visits East Anglia, Lincolnshire and the Midlands. He samples a host of regional specialities including Norfolk black turkeys, Lincolnshire chine, pork pies and Stilton. programme.title: The Pioneer Woman

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver puts his stamp on three comfort food classics. First up is a retro treat - chicken Kiev, before the chef prepares spaghetti vongole with a non-traditional pink twist and a deluxe shepherds pie made with slow-roasted lamb shoulder rather than mince

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten prepares fig and fennel caponata, an antipasto platter and Tuscan mashed chickpeas for the team who built her barn

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten prepares raspberry muffins and coffee for breakfast, before heading to local restaurant South Fork Kitchen

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve contestants begin the battle to be named Britains best amateur baker, facing tasks devised by judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, who have created 30 new tasks to test their baking skills and creativity. Their first signature challenge is to make a Madeira cake, before they attempt to bake a frosted walnut cake in the technical. Day two of the contest sees the contestants going all out to try and impress the judges with their first showstopper, the Black Forest gateau. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins present

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: With the cake week completed, and one of their number already eliminated from the competition, the 11 remaining home bakers must now prove their skill at making biscuits, a task that can be deceptively difficult, and one that has undone many a confident Bake Off contestant in the past. The opening signature challenge this week is to create 24 biscotti, Italian delicacies that must be baked twice to deliver the perfect crisp crunch. Next is Paul Hollywoods technical challenge, in which the contestants tackle Arlettes - labour-intensive, puff pastry-based treats from southwest France. Finally, for the showstopper, the judges demand bespoke edible biscuit boxes, filled with 36 biscuits of different flavours. After all three rounds have concluded, it falls to Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins to announce which of the bakers will be asked to leave the tent

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The chefs culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands, where he takes a tour of the grounds and cooks venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Later, James gets a masterclass in Highland gaming as two young brothers David and Harry share the secrets to the famous caber toss

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Charles and the girls go on a camping trip. and when Caroline scratches herself on a piece of baling wire and develops a dangerous infection in her leg, her family is not around to help. programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Mary, Laura, and Carl wander into a caboose at a train station. Without warning, the caboose begins to move, and it soon is on a crash course with an oncoming train. programme.title: The War Lord (1965)
programme.desc: An 11th-century Norman knight is sent by the duke he serves to defend a druid village. Permitted under feudal law, he takes another mans wife, who falls in love with him and has no intention of returning to her husband. However, her vengeful spouse is outraged and ignites a civil war by influencing the knights brother to turn against him. Medieval drama, with Charlton Heston, Rosemary Forsyth, Richard Boone and Maurice Evans

programme.title: Star Trek
